Discipline · Fire Safety FIRE-002 SEVERITY: CRITICAL

Fire compartmentation plans complete — engineering handover & validation sheet

Everything a software engineer with zero building knowledge needs to automate this check, validated line-by-line against the real drawing set. Yellow boxes are your markup layer — add your own judgment, then export.

Reference projectBianca, 2C–2D Wolseley St, Drummoyne NSW
Architect / JobSJB Architects · 6580
Deliverable checkedAR-2101 → AR-2152 (2100 series)
StandardsNCC 2022 Pt C3 (ex C2.2) · AS 1530.4 · DBP Act 2020
Prepared byValerie Jimenez · 13 Jul 2026
0 comments
Legend — Verdict states (used in every result below)
PASS Positive evidence of compliance found
FAIL Evidence of non-compliance, or the thing is absent
REVIEW Tool can't decide — routed to a human with sheet ref
N/A Rule doesn't apply; reason recorded, doesn't gate

Overall check = worst sub-check (FAIL > REVIEW > PASS). Uncertainty always resolves to REVIEW, never PASS — a wrong FAIL costs an architect ten minutes; a wrong PASS lets a non-compliant fire design into a building people sleep in.

Method

The cheat sheet this document runs on

The framework is check-agnostic; FIRE-002 is one instantiation of it. Everything below the mantra is the same five-step pipeline and six rules, filled in with this client's parameters.

CODE IS UNIVERSAL — THE CLIENT IS A PARAMETER
Table C3D3's limits don't care who drew the set. What varies per client is everything else: the sheet numbering (AR-21xx here, not "A2100"), the legend palette, the compartment ID scheme, the jurisdiction, the stage, the Type of construction. Hard-code the code; parameterise the client.
1 · WHICH?
Which elements does the rule govern
FIRE-002Fire compartments, Class 5–9 only — SOU walls and Class 2 portions are explicitly out
2 · WHERE?
Where each fact lives on the sheets
FIRE-002Levels: GA series · boundaries/IDs: AR-2101–06 · heights: AR-2131 RLs · Type & sprinklers: outside the set
3 · TEST?
The pass/fail sentence with a number
FIRE-002"Compartment area ≤ 8 000 m² AND volume ≤ 48 000 m³ (Class 5, Type A), measured within 2% of annotated"
4 · MISSING?
Missing info = CAN'T-VERIFY, never guess
FIRE-002No Type stated → REVIEW the size check. No sprinkler evidence → REVIEW the carpark exemption
5 · REPORT
Every result cited: sheet + code + fix
FIRE-002"002.4 FAIL · AR-2102 · DBP s.9 auditability · fix: annotate G.1/G.2 areas or add schedule"

A clean PASS with evidence is a deliverable — certainty is the product. The tool never sells a verdict it can't cite.

The 6 rules, instantiated on this set

  1. 1Every step ends PASS / FAIL / CANNOT-VERIFY. Missing info never silently passes. Never guess. HERE → CANNOT-VERIFY is rendered REVIEW in the output contract. The carpark's C3D2 exemption is probably valid — sprinklers are referenced all over the FER notes — but the evidence sheet isn't in the architectural set, so 002.3 says REVIEW, not PASS.
  2. 2Plan vs. schedule vs. detail disagree → that's a FINDING (coordination error), not a coin flip. Say which source governs. HERE → Section AR-2131 labels the basement compartment B1; plan AR-2101 carries no ID at all — reported as a plan/section mismatch, with the plan governing IDs and the section governing heights. Same rule handles register-vs-title-block disagreements (title block wins) and duplicate revisions (latest wins, duplicate reported).
  3. 3Numbers get units and tolerances. HERE → Areas in m², volumes in m³, heights in mm (3375 / 3200 / 3750 from RL subtraction). Measured-vs-annotated disagreement band: 2% → REVIEW. Annotated within 5% of a limit: PASS + near-limit warning. Raster scans double both bands.
  4. 4Gate scope first — what's OUT of the check matters as much as what's in. HERE → Out: SOU separation walls (real fire engineering, wrong rule), Class 2 sizing (no C3D3 entry → N/A branch), FER contents (tool can't evaluate — REVIEW), sprinkler design (other discipline). Step 0 gates the biggest scope of all: the jurisdiction.
  5. 5Think cumulative / system-level. HERE → Compartment 2.4 spans Levels 02–03 and the carpark spans B1→L2 — so volume accumulates across storeys and a per-sheet check would pass walls while missing the system. The stair shaft (120/120/120, B1→L4) only exists as a system, which is why the series includes a section and the tool must read it.
  6. 6Name what the recommended fix breaks. HERE → The fix for the 002.4 FAIL is "annotate every compartment area" — which immediately arms 002.3: declared numbers can expose an over-limit compartment that silence was hiding. If subdividing follows, new fire walls put doors on egress paths (travel-distance and width checks re-open) and every new penetration needs an AR-2151/52 detail. The fix is still right; the report says what it re-opens.
Part A

Pre-flight framing questions, validated against the set

Before any rule runs, four questions scope the check. Each answer below was verified against the actual documents — drawing register AR-0001 (Revs T–Z) and fire series extract AR-2101–2152 — not assumed.

Q1

Which jurisdiction and code edition — any local amendments?

CONFIRMED FROM SETNSW, Australia · NCC 2022 (NSW variations) · DBP Act 2020 overlay

Jurisdiction: New South Wales, Australia. Four independent signals on the sheets agree: the project address (2C–2D Wolseley Street, Drummoyne — Sydney), the consent number in NSW DA format (DA2022/0164, MOD 2024/0036), a Regulated Design Record block with DEP practitioner numbers (exists only under NSW's DBP Act), and the architect entity "SJB Architecture (NSW) Pty Ltd", Surry Hills NSW.

Code edition: NCC 2022. Construction Certificate issues run Jan 2024 → Aug 2025, well after NCC 2022 adoption (1 May 2023 in NSW) — so NCC 2022 with NSW state variations governs. Which exposes a mis-citation in the check itself: "NCC 2022 Part C2, Table C2.2" doesn't exist — 2022 renumbered compartmentation to Part C3, Table C3D3 (limits unchanged). "C2.2" is NCC 2019 numbering. The tool must alias both and cite both, because half the industry still says C2.2.

Local amendments that matter: NSW state variations to the NCC (incl. an NSW-specific C3D6), and the DBP Act 2020 / Regulation 2021 — fire compartmentation is a "regulated design" for any building with a Class 2 part, so every fire sheet must carry a declared practitioner. That's an NSW-only proxy: skip it in other states, don't fail its absence.

AR-0001 title blockRegulated Design Record: John Pradel DEP0000099Body Corp Reg DEP0002963Revs A–O · 30.01.24 → 09.08.25
Q2

What stage is the client's set — CD? Are we enforcing or advising?

NUANCE FOUNDFiled as "CD Set" — but the title blocks say otherwise

The set is past CD. The file is named "CD Set", but every title block reads FOR CONSTRUCTION under a CONSTRUCTION CERTIFICATE staging regime — the register legend maps revisions to CC packages (CC1 excavation & shoring → CC4 remainder of works), and revisions A through O (Jan 2024–Aug 2025) are all "For Construction" issues. This is an issued IFC/CC set, not a design-development snapshot.

Enforcing vs advising follows from that. FIRE-002 is scoped to DD and CD — its natural home is a gate: severity Critical means a FAIL blocks the set from issuing. Run against an already-issued FC set, the same check becomes an audit: findings are advisory, routed to the architect and certifier for the next revision, because the tool has no issue to block. The tool's verdicts don't change — only who acts on them and how fast. The output should therefore carry a mode: enforce | advise flag derived from the title-block status, never hard-coded.

Title block: "FOR CONSTRUCTION"Register legend: CC1–CC4Rev O · 09.08.25 · "For Construction - CC4"
Q3

What does their set actually contain — and what conventions does it use?

INVENTORIED9-sheet fire series; full inventory in Part C below

The fire compartmentation series is nine sheets: six level plans (AR-2101 Basement 01 → AR-2106 Level 04), one overall section (AR-2131), and two passive-fire detail sheets (AR-2151/52). Note the check said "Sheet ref A2100" — no such sheet exists. The real series is AR-21xx; resolving by register title match (FIRE\s*COMPART), never by hard-coded number, is what makes the check portable across firms.

Their conventions: every plan sheet carries the same two-part legend — six FRL boundary colours plus smoke seals, drenched openings, a services fire box (FB) symbol, and three door-rating tags (FR1/FR2/FR3); and a building-class legend (Class 2 / Class 7A hatches + dashed FIRE COMPARTMENT linetype). Compartments are labelled <level>.<n> — G.1, G.2, 1.1–1.2, 2.1–2.4, 3.1–3.3, 4.1. FER references appear as leadered notes. What's absent: not one m² area annotation anywhere in the series — which is exactly what sub-check 4 exists to catch.

Extract pp.5–13Legend on every plan sheetIDs: G.1 → 4.1"REFER TO FER" ×4 sheets
Q4

Your fourth framing question

Your list was truncated after Q3 ("What does their set…"). A strong candidate for the fourth: "What evidence lives outside the architectural set — sprinkler status, Type of construction, the FER — and what does the check do when it can't see it?" (Answer: each missing item degrades its dependent sub-check to REVIEW; the tool asks loudly instead of guessing.) Record your own Q4 and its answer here.

Part B

The four sub-checks — spec, evidence, and verdict in one card each

The one-line check is four independent checks wearing one ID. Each card below is the complete unit: the ask verbatim, what the tool verifies, the drawings it reads, the clause that gives it authority, the verdict from running it by hand on this set, and the copyable validation prompt. Overall check = worst of the four (FAIL > REVIEW > PASS; N/A recorded, doesn’t gate).

Step 0 runs before all four: resolve jurisdiction from the title-block address. Non-Australian project → HALT, never run the numbers (a PASS against the wrong code is worse than no answer). Non-NSW → run NCC sub-checks, skip DBP proxies. Edition pinned by approval dates, not today’s date.
Legend — Reference chips
AR-2102 · p.6 Drawing in the analysed set (sheet · extract page)
NCC 2022 C3D3 Code / regulation clause giving the rule authority
BCA report Evidence outside the architectural set → REVIEW if unconfirmed
002.1

Plans exist for all levels

PASS

“Confirm fire compartmentation plans exist for all levels.” — every level on the GA plan list has a 2100-series fire plan. The level list comes from the GA series, never from the fire series itself — a forgotten level would vanish from the denominator too.

Drawings
AR-0001 register · pp.1–4 (use Rev Z)AR-1101–1108 GA plans (denominator)AR-2101–2106 · pp.5–10AR-2131 · p.11 (RL cross-check)
Code basis
DBP Act 2020 (NSW) ss 6–9 + Reg 2021NCC 2022 C3D3(2)

Fire compartmentation of a building with a Class 2 part is a regulated design: a registered practitioner must prepare and declare it before building work — so the plans must exist as declared documents, per level, at current revision. C3D3(2) exempts roof-only plant levels.

Verdict

PASS — GA series declares B01, Ground, L01–L04 + roof. Fire plans AR-2101–2106 cover B01→L04; the roof is plant-only, so none is required there. Section AR-2131’s RL list independently confirms the same seven levels.

AR-2101–2106AR-2131 RL listRegister AR-0001
Validation prompt — copy into the tool
Validation prompt — 002.1
Build the canonical level list from the GA floor-plan series (register AR-0001,
series 1100), never from the fire series itself. For each level with enclosed
occupiable area, confirm a FIRE COMPARTMENTATION plan exists in the 2100 series
at its latest revision (register: AR-2101 Rev F … AR-2106 Rev F). Cross-check the
level list against the datum RLs on section AR-2131. A missing level = FAIL citing
the GA sheet that establishes it; a roof-only plant level = exempt per NCC 2022
C3D3(2); ambiguous (roof with enclosed rooms) = REVIEW.
002.2

Boundaries shown and labelled

PASS1 REVIEW

“Compartment boundaries shown and labelled.” — four proxies, all required per sheet: legend exists · boundary linework closes · every region carries a class label / compartment ID · boundaries carry FRLs. Palette read from each sheet’s own legend swatches, never hard-coded.

Drawings
AR-2101–2106 · pp.5–10 (legend + linework + IDs)AR-2131 · p.11 (ID cross-check)AR-2151/52 · pp.12–13 (details)AR-4102 (FB detail)
Code basis
NCC 2022 C3D8 + Spec 5AS 1530.4

A boundary is only a boundary if it’s a rated construction — every drawn boundary must carry an FRL, and every FRL traces to an AS 1530.4 furnace test. The tool checks notation exists and is well-formed (values in {30, 60, 90, 120, 180, 240}); it never re-derives ratings.

Verdict

PASS with one flag — full two-part legend on every plan; compartments hatched, class-labelled, ID’d (G.1→4.1); FRLs colour-coded. REVIEW: basement plan has no B.n ID (section says B1) — plan/section mismatch; plus the stray “?” on AR-2131.

Legend ×6 sheetsIDs G.1–4.1AR-2101 unlabelled basementAR-2131 “?”
Validation prompt — copy into the tool
Validation prompt — 002.2
On every 2100-series plan sheet verify four proxies, all required:
(1) a legend cluster titled like FIRE COMPARTMENT LEGEND with at least one
well-formed FRL matching (\d{2,3}|[-–])/(\d{2,3}|[-–])/(\d{2,3}|[-–]),
values in {30,60,90,120,180,240}, whitespace-tolerant;
(2) boundary linework forming closed or wall-terminated chains — read the colour
palette from that sheet's own legend swatches, never hard-coded;
(3) every enclosed region labelled with a building class (CLASS 2 / CLASS 7A)
and/or a compartment ID (scheme here: level.n — G.1…4.1);
(4) every boundary mapped to an FRL via legend colour or leadered tag.
Cross-check plan IDs against section AR-2131; a mismatch = REVIEW.
No legend or no labels at all = FAIL; partial/unclosed/low-confidence = REVIEW.
002.3

Sizes comply with NCC Table C2.2 (= C3D3)

N/AREVIEW

“Maximum compartment sizes comply with NCC Table C2.2.” — each compartment’s area and volume against Table C3D3 (2022 numbering of the cited C2.2 — Part D below), honouring its exemptions. Row = class, column = Type of construction, and both limits must hold.

Drawings
AR-2101–2106 · pp.5–10 (geometry @ 1:100)AR-2131 · p.11 (RLs → storey heights)BCA report (Type)Fire services dwgs (Spec 17 sprinklers)FER (performance solutions)
Code basis
NCC 2022 Table C3D3C3D2(1)C3D4C3D6 (NSW-varied)

Scope is Class 5–9 only — Class 2/3 portions are N/A by C3D3(1); each exemption clause reroutes the verdict rather than relaxing it silently. A DTS breach covered by an FER is REVIEW, never FAIL — the tool can’t evaluate an FER.

Verdict

N/A + REVIEW — Class 2 portions: no table entry, N/A recorded with reason. Class 7A carpark: C3D2 sprinkler exemption likely (FER notes reference sprinklers throughout) but the evidence lives outside this set → REVIEW “confirm Spec 17 coverage”. FER-covered departures stay REVIEW.

C3D3(1) scopeC3D2 exemptionFER notes ×4
Validation prompt — copy into the tool
Validation prompt — 002.3
For each compartment on AR-2101–2106: resolve its class set from hatches/labels.
Class ⊆ {2,3,4} → N/A citing C3D3(1). Class 7a with confirmed Spec 17 sprinkler
or open deck → N/A per C3D2(1); unconfirmed (evidence lives in fire services
dwgs / BCA report, outside this set) → REVIEW. Class 9a patient care → apply
C3D6 sub-limits instead. Otherwise: row = class (pro-rata if mixed), column =
Type of construction from the BCA report (unknown → REVIEW, never guess);
area = annotated value cross-measured from polygon geometry at 1:100
(disagreement > 2% → REVIEW); volume = area × storey height from AR-2131 RLs
(consecutive RL differences: 3375/3200/3200/3200/3200/3750 mm; multi-storey
compartments like 2.4 and the B1→L2 carpark sum their storeys). Over either
limit: FER referenced on sheet → REVIEW; C3D4 plausible → REVIEW; else FAIL
citing the clause, the limit, and both measured and annotated values.
002.4

Areas annotated

FAIL

“Compartment areas annotated.” — every compartment carries exactly one declared m² value, on-sheet or in a schedule, associated to its region by centroid, leader, or schedule row.

Drawings
AR-2101–2106 · pp.5–10 (in-plan text)Any compartment schedule (none exists)
Code basis
DBP Act 2020 s.9 declarationNCC 2022 C3D3 (auditability)

No NCC clause literally says “write the m² on the sheet” — the requirement is evidentiary: the declared design must let the certifier verify the C3D3 limits without re-deriving them. An undeclared area makes the regulated design unauditable.

Verdict

FAIL — a regex sweep of all nine sheets (pp.5–13) finds zero m²/SQM tokens and no schedule. Every compartment fails “exactly one declared area”. This drives the overall verdict, and it is precisely the omission FIRE-002 exists to catch.

pp.5–13 sweep: 0 hits
Validation prompt — copy into the tool
Validation prompt — 002.4
Sweep every 2100-series plan sheet for area tokens matching
[\d,\.]+\s*(m²|m2|sqm) — e.g. "1,234 m²", "1234m2", "AREA: 1,234 SQM".
Associate each token to a compartment by centroid-inside-region, leader line,
or a schedule row keyed by the 002.2 compartment IDs (accept tabulated
schedules as equivalent to in-plan annotation). Every compartment needs
exactly one declared area: zero = FAIL that compartment naming its sheet;
conflicting values = REVIEW. On this set the sweep returns zero hits on all
nine sheets → 002.4 FAIL, driving the overall Critical verdict.
OVERALL: FAIL (CRITICAL) Blocked on 002.4; one human-review packet on 002.3. Actionable path: architect adds area annotations, reviewer confirms the sprinkler statement, set re-runs to PASS. The check said “A2100”, the real sheets are AR-21xx — register-first resolution handled it with no special-casing.
Part C

The evidence — register + nine sheets, annotated

One figure per sheet: the drawing, its revision, its machine-readable findings, and your markup box — nothing repeated elsewhere. Register status: FOR CONSTRUCTION under CC staging (the Q2 nuance).

Legend — Reading the sheets
Register colours Green cell = flagged as DBP regulated design · red cell = included in that CC package
Plan colours Boundary line colours map to FRLs via each sheet’s own legend panel (reproduced below the gallery)
Resolution Thumbnails are 1400 px renders of A1 @ 1:100 — the tool measures the vector PDF, not these images
Legend — As printed on every 2100-series plan sheet (read this per-sheet; never hard-code)
FRL 60/60/60 min
FRL −/60/60 min
FRL 90/90/90 min
FRL −/90/90 min
FRL 120/120/120 min
FRL −/120/120 min
Drenched opening
Smoke seal
Services fire box (FB) → AR-4102
Building class 2 (residential)
Building class 7A (carpark)
Fire compartment boundary
FR1Door −/60/30 + med-temp smoke seals
FR2Door −/120/30
FR3Door −/90/90
Part D

The rule table sub-check 3 enforces

NCC 2022 Table C3D3 (the check's cited "Table C2.2", 2019 numbering — same limits). Every cell is two limits; a compartment must satisfy both.

NCC 2022 Table C3D3 — maximum fire compartment size
ClassificationType A (most fire-resistant)Type BType C (least)
Class 5, 9b or 9c8 000 m² · 48 000 m³5 500 m² · 33 000 m³3 000 m² · 18 000 m³
Class 6, 7, 8 or 9a (except patient-care areas)5 000 m² · 30 000 m³3 500 m² · 21 000 m³2 000 m² · 12 000 m³
Class 2, 3, 4N/A No entry — the table covers Class 5–9 only (C3D3(1)). Your code needs an explicit N/A branch with reason recorded: not PASS-by-silence, not a KeyError.
Legend — Reading the rule table
Row = Class Building use category, printed in the fire-plan legend (this project: Class 2 over Class 7A)
Column = Type Required structural fire resistance (A/B/C), stated in the BCA report — never inferred; unknown → REVIEW
Cell = 2 limits Area AND volume; breaching either fails — no heights available → REVIEW the volume half, don't skip it silently

Exemptions that move the pass line

Exemption routing
ClauseConditionTool response
C3D2(1)Carpark sprinklered per Spec 17 (not FPAA101D/H), or open-deckN/A if confirmed — but sprinkler evidence lives outside the architectural set → REVIEW when unconfirmed
C3D3(2)Rooftop plant roomsExcluded from area/volume; a roof-only level may legitimately have no fire plan
C3D4Large isolated buildings (sprinklers + perimeter brigade access)Over-limit + plausible C3D4 → REVIEW — evidence is drawing-external
C3D6Class 9a patient care / 9c aged careTighter sub-limits (2 000 / 1 000 / 500 m²) replace the table row
—DTS breach with FER / performance solution referenced on sheetREVIEW, never FAIL — the tool cannot evaluate an FER (this set references one for roller shutters, stairs, switch-room sprinkler omission)
Part E

Where the engineer gets stuck (ranked)

Traps, most damaging first
#TrapDefence
1Applying the code without checking the address — NCC numbers against a US/UK project produce confident nonsenseJurisdiction is Step 0, before any sub-check
2Looking up "Table C2.2" in NCC 2022 → wrong Part entirelyAlias C2.2 (2019) ↔ C3D3 (2022); pin edition to approval dates
3Running the table against Class 2 — the commonest NSW building type has no entryExplicit N/A branch; never PASS-by-silence or KeyError
4Hard-coding "A2100" — zero real sets match (this one is AR-21xx)Register-first title match, confirmed against title blocks
5Deriving "all levels" from the fire series itself — circular denominatorLevel list from GA series; roof gets the enclosed-area test
6Forgetting volume — every cell is area AND volumeHeights from AR-2131 RLs; unknown → REVIEW the volume half
7FAILing what an FER legally coversDTS breach + FER reference = REVIEW, always
8Trusting annotations without re-measuring, or vice versa2% disagreement band → REVIEW; near-limit PASS gets a warning
9Processing superseded revisions (register here has T, V, X, Z copies)Dedupe by sheet number, keep max revision, report duplicates
10Hard-coding legend colours — palettes differ per firm and plotRead the swatches from each sheet's own legend
